 {"id":"bd-qorr","title":"Add exact golden coverage for ext4 feature-diagnostics display contract","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T21:00:36.531313918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T21:05:19.860920754Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T21:05:19.858805611Z","close_reason":"Added exact golden coverage for ext4 feature-diagnostics display strings, including a synthetic all-fields-populated contract and a superblock-derived contract covering missing-required and unknown-bit formatting; validated with rch cargo test -p ffs-ext4 feature_diagnostics -- --nocapture, rch cargo check -p ffs-ext4 --all-targets, rch cargo clippy -p ffs-ext4 --all-targets -- -D warnings, and rch cargo fmt --check.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-qpt5","title":"Add SIMD-accelerated checksum computation","description":"# Add SIMD-accelerated checksum computation

## GOAL
Accelerate CRC32C and BLAKE3 checksum computation using SIMD instructions.

## BACKGROUND (Extreme Software Optimization)
Checksum computation is on the hot path:
- Every block read/write verifies checksum
- ARC cache hits still verify (trust but verify)
- RaptorQ symbols have checksums

SIMD can provide 4-16x speedup depending on instruction set.

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. CRC32C Acceleration
```rust
pub fn crc32c_simd(data: &[u8], seed: u32) -> u32 {
    #[cfg(target_arch = "x86_64")]
    {
        if is_x86_feature_detected!("sse4.2") {
            return crc32c_sse42(data, seed);
        }
    }
    #[cfg(target_arch = "aarch64")]
    {
        if is_aarch64_feature_detected!("crc") {
            return crc32c_arm_crc(data, seed);
        }
    }
    crc32c_fallback(data, seed)
}
```

### 2. BLAKE3 (Already SIMD)
- blake3 crate already uses SIMD
- Ensure proper feature flags enabled
- Benchmark to confirm

### 3. Batch Checksum
```rust
/// Compute checksums for multiple blocks in parallel
pub fn batch_checksum(blocks: &[&[u8]], algo: ChecksumAlgo) -> Vec<u32> {
    // Use rayon for multi-block parallelism
    // Use SIMD within each block
}
```

### 4. Feature Detection at Runtime
- Detect CPU features once at startup
- Store in global (or thread-local)
- Dispatch to appropriate implementation

## TESTS
1. Unit: SIMD matches fallback results
2. Unit: All supported CPUs tested (via CI matrix)
3. Benchmark: Before/after comparison
4. Property: Random data, SIMD == fallback

## LOGGING
- Feature detection (info): detected SIMD capabilities
- Batch checksum (trace): block count, duration

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Correctness: SIMD == fallback for all inputs
2. Performance: >2x speedup on supported CPUs
3. Graceful fallback on unsupported CPUs
4. No unsafe code (use safe SIMD wrappers)

## NOTES
Use existing crates:
- crc32c crate has SIMD support
- blake3 crate is already optimized
Main work is ensuring proper feature flags and benchmarking.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:55:14.096946312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T15:05:05.163955333Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T15:05:05.163936237Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["optimization","perf","simd"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:00.533583752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:29.930946133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":207,"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: general
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.8 Runtime Decision Core + 0.19 Evidence Ledger
EV score: 9 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: B
Budgeted mode: controller_tick_ms=1000, policy_timeout_ms=200
Fallback trigger: static config profile

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:32Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-qqowd","title":"add mounted-path btrfs read-only EROFS coverage for fsync fdatasync and fsyncdir","description":"Survey finding: btrfs fuse_e2e currently proves fsync, fdatasync, and fsyncdir success on writable mounts, but the mounted-path read-only rejection contract is not frozen even though ffs-fuse and ffs-core already return EROFS for these sync operations on read-only mounts. Scope: add btrfs read-only mounted-path probes for file sync_all, sync_data, and directory sync_all that assert exact EROFS and unchanged data/dirent state. Acceptance: btrfs FUSE E2E freezes operator-visible read-only sync refusal for fsync, fdatasync, and fsyncdir.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:20:27.269990402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:20:27.269990402Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Scope: add btrfs read-only mounted-path probes for file sync_all, sync_data, and directory sync_all that assert exact EROFS and unchanged data/dirent state. Acceptance: btrfs FUSE E2E freezes operator-visible read-only sync refusal for fsync, fdatasync, and fsyncdir.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:20:27.269990402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:32:36.746576644Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:32:36.744719148Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-qthq","title":"Implement casefold, fscrypt nokey, tree-log wiring, FC wiring, fix P0 gates","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:10:50.730960136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T08:11:06.183636560Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T08:11:06.183168214Z","close_reason":"Session work:
1. Fixed P0 gate blockers (first_inode, journal clippy) - bd-nzv3.1.1, bd-nzv3.1.2
2. Fixed flaky log contract tests (set_default) - bd-nzv3.19
3. ext4 casefold: lookup_in_dir_block_casefold with Unicode lowercase, CASEFOLD removed from rejected
4. ext4 fscrypt: ENCRYPT removed from rejected (nokey mode - show encrypted names as raw bytes)
5. btrfs tree-log wired into mount path (detect + log, items not applied in RO mode)
6. ext4 fast commit wiring documented in mount path (FC engine exists, full wiring deferred)
7. Partial orphan test fix (s_desc_size + free counts in test image)
FEATURE_PARITY: 84/84 tracked.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-qy1s","title":"Implement spec integration scenarios I-3, I-5, I-6, I-7 as FUSE E2E tests","description":"Spec 17.2 mandates: I-3 write+remount persistence, I-5 delete reclaim free space, I-6 concurrent writes, I-7 MVCC snapshot isolation. None covered by E2E tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T06:21:18.172693136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T19:15:30.448715126Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T19:15:30.448689798Z","close_reason":"All four integration scenarios (I-3, I-5, I-6, I-7) already implemented as FUSE E2E tests: fuse_spec_i3_write_and_persist_after_remount, fuse_spec_i5_delete_reclaims_free_counts, fuse_spec_i6_concurrent_writes_no_corruption, fuse_spec_i7_reader_sees_pre_modification_version_during_write","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -159,7 +159,7 @@ behavior of that operation, not whether the row is unimplemented.
 | FUSE readdir | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | `FsOps::readdir` via `OpenFs` |
 | FUSE read | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | `FsOps::read` via `OpenFs` |
 | FUSE readlink | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | `FsOps::readlink` via `OpenFs` |
-| FUSE mount runtime | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | Production runtime lifecycle, signal handling, dispatch coverage, and CI-safe skip behavior are implemented; OQ4 (`bd-h6nz.6.4`) is resolved for V1.x with explicit `flush` (non-durable) vs `fsync`/`fsyncdir` (durability boundary) contract and writeback-cache-disabled policy. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now emits `SCENARIO_RESULT` coverage for ext4 (`ext4_rw_flush`, `ext4_rw_fdatasync`, `ext4_rw_fsync`, `ext4_rw_fsyncdir`, `ext4_rw_seek_data_hole`) and btrfs (`btrfs_rw_flush`, `btrfs_rw_fdatasync`, `btrfs_rw_fsync`, `btrfs_rw_fsyncdir`, `btrfs_rw_seek_data_hole`) through real file flushes, file- and directory-FD sync calls, and punched-hole `SEEK_DATA`/`SEEK_HOLE` probes over FUSE with EOF `ENXIO` verification. |
+| FUSE mount runtime | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | Production runtime lifecycle, signal handling, dispatch coverage, and CI-safe skip behavior are implemented; OQ4 (`bd-h6nz.6.4`) is resolved for V1.x with explicit `flush` (non-durable) vs `fsync`/`fsyncdir` (durability boundary) contract and writeback-cache-disabled policy. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now emits `SCENARIO_RESULT` coverage for ext4 (`ext4_rw_flush`, `ext4_rw_fdatasync`, `ext4_rw_fsync`, `ext4_rw_fsyncdir`, `ext4_rw_seek_data_hole`) and btrfs (`btrfs_rw_flush`, `btrfs_rw_fdatasync`, `btrfs_rw_fsync`, `btrfs_rw_fsyncdir`, `btrfs_rw_seek_data_hole`) through real file flushes, file- and directory-FD sync calls, and punched-hole `SEEK_DATA`/`SEEK_HOLE` probes over FUSE with EOF `ENXIO` verification; btrfs read-only mounted-path `fsync`, `fdatasync`, and `fsyncdir` now also freeze exact `EROFS` with no data or dirent drift. |
 | FUSE ioctl FIEMAP | `include/linux/fiemap.h` | ✅ | `FsOps::fiemap` queries ext4 extent tree via `collect_extents_with_scope` and btrfs extent data items via `btrfs_fiemap_extent_items`. FUSE `ioctl` handler parses `FS_IOC_FIEMAP` (0xC020660B), rejects unsupported request flags with `EBADR`, honors `FIEMAP_FLAG_SYNC` by fsyncing writable mounts before extent lookup, and marshals the fiemap header plus extent array. `crates/ffs-core` now has deterministic btrfs coverage for inline extents (`physical=0`, `FIEMAP_EXTENT_LAST`) and keep-size prealloc extents (`FIEMAP_EXTENT_UNWRITTEN`), while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` verifies mounted-path FIEMAP reporting for ext4 regular files, ext4 `SYNC` and invalid-request-flag contracts, plus btrfs regular, inline, and keep-size prealloc files. Note: FUSE transport may not deliver this ioctl to userspace on all kernel versions. |
 | FUSE ioctl EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS | `fs/ext4/ioctl.c` | ✅ | `FsOps::get_inode_flags` returns raw ext4 `i_flags` field. FUSE `ioctl` handler dispatches `EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS` (0x80086601). Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now verifies mounted-path `GETFLAGS` on a writable ext4 file. |
 | FUSE ioctl EXT4_IOC_GETVERSION / EXT4_IOC_SETVERSION | `fs/ext4/ioctl.c` | ✅ | `FsOps::get_inode_generation` returns ext4 `i_generation`, and `FsOps::set_inode_generation` now updates that field in-place, persists it via `ffs_inode::write_inode`, and keeps the ext4-only contract explicit. `ffs-fuse` dispatches `EXT4_IOC_GETVERSION` (0x80086603) and `EXT4_IOC_SETVERSION` (0x40086604), enforces the 4-byte userspace payload contract, and commits `SETVERSION` through the normal write request scope. `crates/ffs-core` unit tests cover direct ext4 mutation plus reopen persistence, `crates/ffs-fuse` unit tests cover getter encoding and setter routing, and `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now proves mounted-path `SETVERSION`/`GETVERSION` roundtrips when the current kernel/FUSE stack forwards the ioctl. The mounted-path test soft-skips with explicit scenario results when the kernel/VFS rejects `GETVERSION` or `SETVERSION` before userspace dispatch. |
